member.name: T:Wcwidth.UnicodeCalculator
member.summary: A utility for calculating the width of Unicode characters.

member.name: M:Wcwidth.UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(System.Int32,System.Nullable{Wcwidth.Unicode})
member.summary: Gets the width of a Unicode code point.
member.param: The Unicode code point to calculate the width of.
member.returns: The width of the character (-1, 0, 1, 2).

member.name: M:Wcwidth.UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(System.Text.Rune,System.Nullable{Wcwidth.Unicode})
member.summary: Gets the width of a Unicode scalar.
member.param: The Unicode scalar to calculate the width of.
member.returns: The width of the character (-1, 0, 1, 2).

member.name: M:Wcwidth.UnicodeCalculator.GetWidth(System.Char,System.Nullable{Wcwidth.Unicode})
member.summary: Gets the width of a UTF-16 code unit.
member.param: The UTF-16 code unit to calculate the width of.
member.returns: The width of the character (-1, 0, 1, 2).

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.AnsiCapabilities.ColorSystem
member.summary: Gets or sets the color system.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.AnsiCapabilities.Ansi
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the console supports VT/ANSI control codes.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.AnsiCapabilities.Links
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the console support links.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.AnsiCapabilities.AlternateBuffer
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the console supports alternate buffers.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiCapabilities.Create(System.IO.TextWriter)
member.summary: Creates a instance from the provided arguments.
member.returns: A instance.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiCapabilities.Create(System.IO.TextWriter,Spectre.Console.AnsiWriterSettings)
member.summary: Creates a instance from the provided arguments.
member.returns: A instance.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.IReadOnlyAnsiCapabilities.ColorSystem
member.summary: Gets or sets the color system.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.IReadOnlyAnsiCapabilities.Ansi
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the console supports VT/ANSI control codes.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.IReadOnlyAnsiCapabilities.Links
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the console support links.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.IReadOnlyAnsiCapabilities.AlternateBuffer
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the console supports alternate buffers.

member.name: T:Spectre.Console.AnsiMarkup
member.summary: Utility used for working with markup text.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiMarkup.#ctor(Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The ANSI writer to use for writing.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiMarkup.Write(System.String,System.Nullable{Spectre.Console.Style})
member.param: The base style to use during parsing.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiMarkup.WriteLine(System.String)
member.summary: Outputs the specified markup, followed by the current line terminator

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiMarkup.Parse(System.String,System.Nullable{Spectre.Console.Style})
member.summary: Parses the specified markup text into segments that can be processed.
member.param: The base style to use when parsing.
member.returns: One or more segments that represents the parsed markup.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiMarkup.Escape(System.String)
member.summary: Escapes the specified text so that it won’t be interpreted as markup.
member.returns: The escaped markup.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiMarkup.Remove(System.String)
member.summary: Removes markup from the specified text.
member.returns: The provided text without markup.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiMarkup.Highlight(System.String,System.String,Spectre.Console.Style)
member.summary: Highlights the first text match in provided markup.
member.returns: Highlighted markup text, using the specified style.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiMarkupSegment.#ctor(System.String,Spectre.Console.Style,Spectre.Console.Link)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.AnsiSupport.Detect
member.summary: ANSI escape sequence support should be detected by the system.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.AnsiSupport.No
member.summary: ANSI escape sequences are not supported.

member.name: T:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter
member.summary: Represents an ANSI writer, capable of outputting ANSI/VT escape sequences.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.Capabilities
member.summary: Gets or sets the capabilities for the writer.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.#ctor(System.IO.TextWriter)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.#ctor(System.IO.TextWriter,Spectre.Console.AnsiCapabilities)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.Write(System.String)
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.Write(System.Int32)
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.Write(System.String,Spectre.Console.Style,Spectre.Console.Link)
member.summary: Writes the specified text with the specified style.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.WriteLine
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.WriteLine(System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified text, followed by the current line terminator.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.WriteLine(System.String,Spectre.Console.Style,Spectre.Console.Link)
member.summary: Writes the specified text with the specified style, followed by the current line terminator.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.Style(Spectre.Console.Style,Spectre.Console.Link)
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.ResetStyle
member.summary: Resets any foreground, background, decoration, or style by emitting SGR(0).
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.Decoration(Spectre.Console.Decoration)
member.summary: Sets the current decoration by emitting SGR.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.Background(Spectre.Console.Color)
member.summary: Sets the current background color by emitting SGR.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.Foreground(Spectre.Console.Color)
member.summary: Sets the current foreground color by emitting SGR.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.BeginLink(Spectre.Console.Link)
member.summary: Begins a link by emitting OSC 8.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.BeginLink(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Begins a link by emitting OSC 8.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.EndLink
member.summary: Ends a link by emitting OSC 8.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorPosition(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: This control function moves the cursor to the specified line and column (1-indexed) by emitting CSI row;column H.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorHome
member.summary: Moves the cursor to position 1,1 (top left corner) by emitting CSI H.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorUp(System.Int32)
member.summary: Moves the cursor up a specified number of lines in the same column by emitting CSI n A. The cursor stops at the top margin. If the cursor is already above the top margin, then the cursor stops at the top line.
member.param: The number of steps to move the cursor up.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorDown(System.Int32)
member.summary: This control function moves the cursor down a specified number of lines in the same column by emitting CSI n B. The cursor stops at the bottom margin. If the cursor is already below the bottom margin, then the cursor stops at the bottom line.
member.param: The number of steps to move the cursor down.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorRight(System.Int32)
member.summary: This control function moves the cursor to the right by a specified number of columns by emitting CSI n C. The cursor stops at the right border of the page.
member.param: The number of steps to move the cursor right.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorForward(System.Int32)
member.summary: This control function moves the cursor to the right by a specified number of columns by emitting CSI n C. The cursor stops at the right border of the page.
member.param: The number of steps to move the cursor right.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorLeft(System.Int32)
member.summary: This control function moves the cursor to the left by a specified number of columns by emitting CSI n D. The cursor stops at the left border of the page.
member.param: The number of steps to move the cursor left.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorBackward(System.Int32)
member.summary: This control function moves the cursor to the left by a specified number of columns by emitting CSI n D. The cursor stops at the left border of the page.
member.param: The number of steps to move the cursor left.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.ShowCursor
member.summary: Shows the cursor by emitting CSI ? 25 h.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.HideCursor
member.summary: Hides the cursor by emitting CSI ? 25 l.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.SaveCursor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Saves current cursor position for SCO console mode by emitting CSI s (SCOSC) if staying on page, otherwise ESC 7 (DECSC).
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.RestoreCursor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Moves cursor to the position saved by save cursor command in SCO console mode by emitting CSI u (SCORC) if staying on page, otherwise ESC 8 (DECRC).
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorHorizontalAbsolute(System.Int32)
member.summary: Moves the active position to the n-th character of the active line by emitting CSI n G

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.EnterAltScreen
member.summary: Enters the alternative screen buffer by emitting CSI ? 1049 h.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.ExitAltScreen
member.summary: Exits the alternative screen buffer by emitting CSI ? 1049 l.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.EraseInLine(System.Int32)
member.summary: This control function erases characters on the line that has the cursor. EL clears all character attributes from erased character positions. EL works inside or outside the scrolling margins.
member.param:
The section of the line to erase.
0 From the cursor through the end of the line.
1 From the beginning of the line through the cursor.
2 The complete line.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.EraseInDisplay(System.Int32)
member.summary: This control function erases characters from part or all of the display. When you erase complete lines, they become single-height, single-width lines, with all visual character attributes cleared. ED works inside or outside the scrolling margins.
member.param:
The amount of the display to erase.
0 From the cursor through the end of the display.
1 From the beginning of the display through the cursor.
2 The complete display.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.ClearScrollback
member.summary: Clears the scrollback buffer by emitting CSI 3J.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorBackwardTabulation(System.Int32)
member.summary: Move the active position n tabs backward by emitting CSI n Z.
member.param: The number of tabs to move backwards
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorHorizontalTabulation(System.Int32)
member.summary: Move the active position n tabs forward by emitting CSI n I.
member.param: The number of tabs to move forward
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorNextLine(System.Int32)
member.summary: Move the cursor to the next line by emitting CSI n E.
member.param: The number of lines to move
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.CursorPreviousLine(System.Int32)
member.summary: Move the cursor to the preceding line by emitting CSI n F.
member.param: The number of lines to move
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.Index
member.summary: Moves the cursor down one line in the same column by emitting ESC D. If the cursor is at the bottom margin, then the screen performs a scroll-up.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.ReverseIndex
member.summary: Moves the cursor up one line in the same column by emitting ESC M. If the cursor is at the top margin, then the screen performs a scroll-down.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.DeleteCharacter(System.Int32)
member.summary: This control function deletes one or more characters from the cursor position to the right by emitting CSI n P.
member.param: The number of characters to delete. If is greater than the number of characters between the cursor and the right margin, then DCH only deletes the remaining characters.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.SetCursorStyle(System.Int32)
member.summary: Select the style of the cursor on the screen by emitting CSI n SP q
member.param:
The style of the cursor
0 Terminal default.
1 Blinking block.
2 Steady block.
3 Blinking underline.
5 Steady underline.
6 Steady vertical bar.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.DeleteLine(System.Int32)
member.summary: This control function deletes one or more lines in the scrolling region by emitting CSI n M, starting with the line that has the cursor. As lines are deleted, lines below the cursor and in the scrolling region move up. The terminal adds blank lines with no visual character attributes at the bottom of the scrolling region. If lines is greater than the number of lines remaining on the page, DL deletes only the remaining lines. DL has no effect outside the scrolling margins.
member.param: The number of lines to delete.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.EraseCharacter(System.Int32)
member.summary: Erases one or more characters from the cursor position to the right by emitting CSI n X (ECH). ECH clears character attributes from erased character positions. ECH works inside or outside the scrolling margins.
member.param: The number of characters to erase. A value of 0 or 1 erases one character.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.InsertCharacter(System.Int32)
member.summary: inserts one or more space (SP) characters starting at the cursor position by emitting CSI n @ (ICH). The ICH sequence inserts blank characters with the normal character attribute. The cursor remains at the beginning of the blank characters. Text between the cursor and right margin moves to the right. Characters scrolled past the right margin are lost. ICH has no effect outside the scrolling margins.
member.param: The number of characters to insert.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.InsertLine(System.Int32)
member.summary: Inserts one or more blank lines, starting at the cursor by emitting CSI n L (IL). As lines are inserted, lines below the cursor and in the scrolling region move down. Lines scrolled off the page are lost. IL has no effect outside the page margins.
member.param: The number of lines to insert.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.ScrollDown(System.Int32)
member.summary: Moves the user window up a specified number of lines in page memory by emitting CSI n T.
member.param: The number of lines to move the user window up in page memory. lines new lines appear at the top of the display. lines old lines disappear at the bottom of the display. You cannot pan past the top margin of the current page.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter.ScrollUp(System.Int32)
member.summary: Moves the user window down a specified number of lines in page memory by emitting CSI n S.
member.param: The number of lines to move the user window down in page memory. lines new lines appear at the bottom of the display. lines old lines disappear at the top of the display. You cannot pan past the bottom margin of the current page.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriterSettings.Ansi
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not ANSI escape sequences are supported.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.AnsiWriterSettings.ColorSystem
member.summary: Gets or sets the color system to use.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.Color.Number
member.summary: Gets the number of the color, if any.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.Color.IsDefault
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether or not this is the default color.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.#ctor(System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the struct.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.Blend(Spectre.Console.Color,System.Single)
member.returns: The resulting color.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.ToHex
member.summary: Gets the hexadecimal representation of the color.
member.returns: The hexadecimal representation of the color.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.ExactOrClosest(Spectre.Console.ColorSystem)
member.summary: Gets the exact or closest color in the specified .
member.returns: The exact or closest color in the specified .

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.op_Equality(Spectre.Console.Color,Spectre.Console.Color)
member.summary: Checks if two instances are equal.
member.param: The first color instance to compare.
The second color instance to compare.
member.returns: true if the two colors are equal, otherwise false.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.op_Inequality(Spectre.Console.Color,Spectre.Console.Color)
member.summary: Checks if two instances are not equal.
member.param: The first color instance to compare.
The second color instance to compare.
member.returns: true if the two colors are not equal, otherwise false.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.ToConsoleColor(Spectre.Console.Color)
member.returns: A representing the .

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.FromInt32(System.Int32)
member.summary: Converts a color number into a .
member.returns: The color representing the specified color number.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.FromHex(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a color from a hexadecimal string representation.
member.param: The hexadecimal string representation of the color.
member.returns: The color created from the hexadecimal string.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.TryFromHex(System.String,Spectre.Console.Color@)
member.summary: Tries to convert a hexadecimal color code to a object.
member.param: When this method returns, contains the equivalent of the hexadecimal color code, if the conversion succeeded, or if the conversion failed.
member.returns: true if the conversion succeeded; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.FromName(System.String)
member.returns: The requested or null if not found.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.FromConsoleColor(System.ConsoleColor)
member.returns: A representing the .

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Color.ToMarkup
member.summary: Converts the color to a markup string.
member.returns: A representing the color as markup.

member.name: T:Spectre.Console.ColorSystemSupport
member.summary: Determines what color system should be used.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.ColorSystemSupport.Detect
member.summary: Try to detect the color system.

member.name: T:Spectre.Console.Decoration
member.remarks: Support for text decorations is up to the terminal.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Bold
member.summary: Bold text. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Dim
member.summary: Dim or faint text. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Italic
member.summary: Italic text. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Underline
member.summary: Underlined text. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Invert
member.summary: Swaps the foreground and background colors. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Conceal
member.summary: Hides the text. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.SlowBlink
member.summary: Makes text blink. Normally less than 150 blinks per minute. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.RapidBlink
member.summary: Makes text blink. Normally more than 150 blinks per minute. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: F:Spectre.Console.Decoration.Strikethrough
member.summary: Shows text with a horizontal line through the center. Not supported in every environment.

member.name: P:Spectre.Console.Style.Plain
member.summary: Gets a with the default colors and without text decoration.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Style.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Style.#ctor(System.Nullable{Spectre.Console.Color},System.Nullable{Spectre.Console.Color},System.Nullable{Spectre.Console.Decoration})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Style.Combine(Spectre.Console.Style)
member.summary: Combines this style with another one.
member.param: The item to combine with this.
member.returns: A new style representing a combination of this and the other one.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Style.op_Implicit(Spectre.Console.Color)~Spectre.Console.Style
member.summary: Implicitly converts into a with a foreground color.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Style.Parse(System.String)
member.summary: Converts the string representation of a style to its equivalent.
member.param: A string containing a style to parse.
member.returns: A equivalent of the text contained in .

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Style.TryParse(System.String,Spectre.Console.Style@)
member.summary: Converts the string representation of a style to its equivalent. A return value indicates whether the operation succeeded.
member.param: A string containing a style to parse.
When this method returns, contains the equivalent of the text contained in , if the conversion succeeded, or null if the conversion failed.
member.returns: true if s was converted successfully; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.Style.ToMarkup
member.summary: Returns the markup representation of this style.
member.returns: The markup representation of this style.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.StyleExtensions.Foreground(Spectre.Console.Style,Spectre.Console.Color)
member.summary: Creates a new style from the specified one with the specified foreground color.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.StyleExtensions.Background(Spectre.Console.Style,Spectre.Console.Color)
member.summary: Creates a new style from the specified one with the specified background color.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.StyleExtensions.Decoration(Spectre.Console.Style,Spectre.Console.Decoration)
member.summary: Creates a new style from the specified one with the specified text decoration.
member.returns: The same instance so that multiple calls can be chained.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.SystemConsoleExtensions.<G>$BAD8AF7B4AB3B102ABB1C587EA783F40.Ansi(System.Action{Spectre.Console.AnsiWriter})
member.summary: Write ANSI, using the default instance.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.SystemConsoleExtensions.<G>$BAD8AF7B4AB3B102ABB1C587EA783F40.Markup(System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified markup to the console, using the default instance.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.SystemConsoleExtensions.<G>$BAD8AF7B4AB3B102ABB1C587EA783F40.MarkupLine(System.String)
member.summary: Writes the specified markup to the console, followed by the current line terminator, using the default instance.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.StringExtensions.EscapeMarkup(System.String)
member.summary: Escapes text so that it won’t be interpreted as markup.
member.returns: A string that is safe to use in markup.

member.name: M:Spectre.Console.StringExtensions.RemoveMarkup(System.String)
member.summary: Removes markup from the specified string.
member.param: The text to remove markup from.
member.returns: A string that does not have any markup.
